title: Weak-acid pH: solve the ICE table for [H‚Āļ]
blurb: A weak acid only partly ionizes, so its pH is not simply ‚ąílog of its concentration ‚ÄĒ you have to solve the equilibrium. Each drill gives a weak acid, its K‚āź, and how concentrated the solution is; you find the pH the real way, from the ICE table's mass-action root (the same solver the acetic-acid lesson uses). Type your answer (rounded to three significant figures). If you slip, the drill names the mistake ‚ÄĒ treating the weak acid as strong (pH = ‚ąílog[HA]‚āÄ), confusing K‚āź with the hydrogen-ion concentration (that gives the pK‚āź), or forgetting the square root in [H‚Āļ] = ‚ąö(K‚āź¬∑[HA]‚āÄ). The arithmetic is machine-checked; the equilibrium model is disclosed, not proved, and K‚āź is a sourced measured value.
skills: set up the ICE table for HA ‚áĆ H‚Āļ + A‚ĀĽ and solve K‚āź = [H‚Āļ]¬≤/([HA]‚āÄ ‚ąí [H‚Āļ]) for [H‚Āļ]
recognize that a weak acid ionizes only slightly, so pH ‚Č† ‚ąílog[HA]‚āÄ
keep K‚āź (the equilibrium constant) distinct from [H‚Āļ] (what you solve for)

problems.prompt: A 0.2 M solution of hydrocyanic acid (HCN) has K‚āź = 4.9√ó10‚ĀĽ¬Ļ‚Āį. What is the pH? (Solve HCN ‚áĆ H‚Āļ + A‚ĀĽ for [H‚Āļ], then pH = ‚ąílog‚āĀ‚āÄ[H‚Āļ].)

chain.note: [H‚Āļ] from K‚āź = [H‚Āļ][A‚ĀĽ]/[HCN] (the extent where Q = K‚āź)
pH = ‚ąílog‚āĀ‚āÄ[H‚Āļ]

diagnostics.misconception: Treated the weak acid as strong (fully ionized), so [H‚Āļ] = the formal concentration 0.2 M and pH = ‚ąílog[HA]‚āÄ. Only a small fraction of a WEAK acid ionizes.
Confused K‚āź with [H‚Āļ]: pH = ‚ąílog(K‚āź) is the pK‚āź, not the pH. K‚āź sets the equilibrium; you still solve for [H‚Āļ].
Dropped the square root ‚ÄĒ [H‚Āļ] = ‚ąö(K‚āź¬∑[HA]‚āÄ) for a weak acid (from K‚āź ‚Čą [H‚Āļ]¬≤/[HA]‚āÄ), not K‚āź¬∑[HA]‚āÄ.

problems.explain: Set up the ICE table for HCN ‚áĆ H‚Āļ + A‚ĀĽ and solve K‚āź = [H‚Āļ]¬≤/([HA]‚āÄ ‚ąí [H‚Āļ]) for the extent [H‚Āļ] = 0.0000099 M. Then pH = ‚ąílog‚āĀ‚āÄ[H‚Āļ] = 5. Only 0.0049% of the acid ionizes ‚ÄĒ it is weak.

assumptions.claim: One dominant equilibrium: only the acid's ionization matters, and water's own autoionization is neglected ([H‚Āļ]‚āÄ = 0). Valid when the acid's [H‚Āļ] ‚Čę 10‚ĀĽ‚Ā∑.
Activities are approximated by molar concentrations ‚ÄĒ the ideal-dilute-solution model, which is why K‚āź is written with concentrations.
